sentence_reviews.text: Training targets are constructed from the data itself, such as masked regions, future tokens, or paired views.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: SimCLR arXiv abstract describes contrastive self-supervised learning and predictive tasks (lines 41-44); JEPA arXiv abstract describes predicting representations of target blocks in the same image from a context block (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected sources support paired-view and masked/target-block self-supervised targets; future-token prediction is not directly evidenced by these sources.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Self-supervised learning is to connect Unsupervised learning, Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units to downstream capabilities including Contrastive learning, Predictive representation learning / JEPA, In-context & test-time learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: SimCLR arXiv abstract on contrastive self-supervised representation learning (lines 41-44); JEPA arXiv abstract on self-supervised image representation learning (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support links to contrastive learning and JEPA, but not tokens/vocabularies or in-context/test-time learning.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: SimCLR arXiv abstract calls SimCLR a framework for contrastive learning and discusses contrastive self-supervised learning algorithms (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: The source directly connects contrastive and self-supervised representation learning; 'substrate' is atlas wording.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Predictive representation learning / JEPA
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: JEPA arXiv abstract introduces I-JEPA as a non-generative self-supervised approach that predicts target-block representations from context (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: The source directly identifies JEPA as self-supervised predictive representation learning.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: SimCLR arXiv abstract (lines 41-44); JEPA arXiv abstract (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected evidence does not support in-context or test-time learning as downstream of self-supervised learning.

sentence_reviews.text: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Unsupervised learning, Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: SimCLR arXiv abstract notes design choices for augmentations and predictive tasks (lines 41-44); JEPA arXiv abstract notes masking strategy design choices (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support that task/masking design matters, but not the deployment-objective warning or token dependency.

sentence_reviews.text: Self-supervised learning follows the learning setup expressed by Unsupervised learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: SimCLR arXiv abstract discusses unsupervised and self-supervised training on ImageNet (lines 41-44); JEPA arXiv abstract calls I-JEPA self-supervised (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: Self-supervised learning is commonly framed as unsupervised representation learning, but the exact dependency statement is synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Self-supervised learning uses Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: SimCLR arXiv abstract (lines 41-44); JEPA arXiv abstract (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected sources are image/vision self-supervised sources and do not support a tokens/vocabulary dependency.

sentence_reviews.text: Self-supervised learning uses Sequences, events & time series as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: SimCLR arXiv abstract (lines 41-44); JEPA arXiv abstract (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected evidence does not establish a sequence/time-series dependency.

sentence_reviews.text: Self-supervised learning uses Images, video & spatial grids as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: SimCLR arXiv abstract for visual representations (lines 41-44); JEPA arXiv abstract for image representations and image target blocks (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected sources directly support image-based self-supervised learning.

sentence_reviews.text: Self-supervised learning needs sampled data batches to construct masking, pairing, or prediction targets from the observed data itself.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: SimCLR arXiv abstract discusses data augmentations, batch size, and contrastive prediction tasks (lines 41-44); JEPA arXiv abstract discusses sampling target and context blocks (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources directly support sampled augmented pairs/batches and target/context block sampling as part of self-supervised target construction.

edge_reviews.notes: The relation is defensible as self-supervised representation learning without manual labels, but the exact trained_under edge is an atlas convention.
The inspected image-focused evidence does not support a token/vocabulary dependency.
The inspected evidence does not support a sequence/time-series dependency for the general node.
The inspected sources directly use images/spatial target blocks for self-supervised learning.
Batching, augmentation pairs, and sampled target/context blocks directly support this core uses edge.
